rationale: Round-1 gap fully closed. Built the binary at 989e2684 and ran `panel create demo-slug --spec 999-demo --target main` in a scratch /tmp repo: stdout now emits a second line `panel directory: <dir>` with the exact absolute resolved path (verified the dir exists on disk with panel.json+BRIEF.md at that path). Re-ran with `--round 2` (re-panel): the same correct directory line reprints, unchanged, confirming stability across re-panels. interfaces.md's `panel create` section now documents this as a two-line stdout contract and explicitly frames the second line as 'the stable, greppable contract a caller ... reads to learn the panel directory without re-deriving the layout logic' — exactly closing the 111 Bead 2 coordination gap I flagged in round 1. Grammar/exit-code/verify-tally findings from round 1 stand unchanged and are unaffected by this delta.
findings: Empirically confirmed: fresh `panel create` and a `--round 2` re-panel both print `panel directory: <absolute dir>` as the final stdout line, matching the real on-disk location.
interfaces.md's panel create prose now states the two-line stdout contract verbatim and names it as the capture surface for callers — coherent doc-sync for the next bead/spec 111.

Minor, non-blocking observation: no dedicated Go test pins the literal `panel directory: ` stdout line itself (the round-2 test delta only adds C1-control-byte cases to the R5 test); a future regression could silently drop or reword this line without any test failing. Not required for this round given the manual/BRIEF verification discipline already covers it, but worth a cheap follow-up assertion if this file is touched again.
